extraction.one_line_summary: YATES Simon won the technical Budapest time trial by 3 seconds over VAN DER POEL Mathieu, executing the uphill finish climb better than the pink jersey.
tactical_patterns: YATES Simon gained decisive margin on final 1.3km climb despite being marginally slower than VAN DER POEL at climb base.
DUMOULIN Tom's time trial form resurged after long absence, beating climbing specialists by 5 seconds before final climb negated advantage.
KÄMNA Lennard showed unexpected TT prowess on technical parcours, beating established specialist AFFINI Edoardo despite minimal professional TT pedigree.
SOBRERO Matteo's Italian championship form held against world-class field until pure power specialists arrived, suggesting technical courses suit him.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: career-best time trial on technical urban course with uphill finish suggests new dimension beyond climbing specialty
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: retained 11-second GC lead despite losing stage, demonstrating TT competence limits vulnerability in future short efforts
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: third place only 5 seconds down signals genuine return to form after extended absence and injury struggles
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: positive: beat established TT specialists without professional pedigree suggests punchy technical parcours unlock hidden strength
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: third fastest among GC group despite age and declining TT reputation, tactical implications for mountain stage timegaps
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: 30 seconds down represents solid damage limitation but reveals TT remains exploitable weakness against pure specialists
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: 16 seconds down places him among elite TT group, stronger showing than climbing reputation suggests
rider_signals.TULETT Ben: positive: grand tour time trial debut performance mentioned alongside elite results suggests breakthrough talent identification moment
